name: Hypertension (essential)
summary: Chronically elevated blood pressure (systolic ≥130 or diastolic ≥80 mmHg). Major risk factor for stroke, heart disease, and kidney disease. Often asymptomatic; diagnosis confirmed with multiple readings on different days.
symptoms: Often silent. May present with headache, dizziness, blurred vision, or epistaxis at very high readings.
first_steps: Lifestyle: low-sodium diet (DASH), 150 min/week moderate exercise, weight loss, alcohol moderation. First-line meds: thiazide, ACE inhibitor, ARB, or calcium-channel blocker.
red_flags: BP >180/120 with chest pain, neurologic deficit, or vision change → hypertensive emergency, ER now.

name: Type 2 diabetes mellitus
summary: Insulin resistance with relative insulin deficiency. Diagnosed by HbA1c ≥6.5%, fasting glucose ≥126 mg/dL, or 2-h OGTT ≥200 mg/dL. Lifelong condition; complications include retinopathy, nephropathy, neuropathy, CV disease.
symptoms: Polyuria, polydipsia, fatigue, blurred vision, slow-healing wounds. Often discovered on routine labs.
first_steps: Metformin first-line. Lifestyle: weight loss 5-10%, low-glycemic diet, 150 min/week activity. Add GLP-1 RA or SGLT2i if CV/renal risk.
red_flags: HHS or DKA: severe hyperglycemia + altered mental status → ER.

name: Migraine without aura
summary: Recurrent unilateral, throbbing, moderate-to-severe headaches lasting 4-72h, often with nausea/photophobia. Common in women; frequently misdiagnosed as tension headache.
symptoms: Pulsating one-sided pain, nausea, vomiting, sensitivity to light/sound, worsened by routine activity.
first_steps: Acute: NSAID + triptan. Preventive (≥4 attacks/mo): propranolol, topiramate, or amitriptyline. Avoid triggers (sleep deprivation, dehydration, alcohol).
red_flags: Sudden 'thunderclap' headache, fever + neck stiffness, focal deficits → urgent imaging / ER.

name: Tension-type headache
summary: Bilateral, pressing/tightening, mild-to-moderate headache. Most common headache type. Triggered by stress, poor posture, sleep loss, or eyestrain.
symptoms: Bandlike pressure around the head, no nausea/photophobia (or only mild), not worsened by activity.
first_steps: Acetaminophen or NSAID; address triggers. Chronic (≥15 days/mo) → amitriptyline preventive, behavioral therapy.
red_flags: Sudden severe headache, neurologic deficit, fever, papilledema → urgent eval.

name: GERD (gastroesophageal reflux disease)
summary: Chronic acid reflux causing heartburn or regurgitation ≥2/week. Risk factors: obesity, hiatal hernia, smoking, late meals.
symptoms: Burning chest pain after meals, sour taste, regurgitation, chronic cough, hoarseness.
first_steps: Lifestyle: weight loss, head-of-bed elevation, avoid late meals, reduce alcohol/caffeine. PPI 8 weeks; H2 blocker if mild.
red_flags: Dysphagia, weight loss, GI bleeding, anemia → endoscopy to rule out malignancy/Barrett's.

name: Asthma — mild persistent
summary: Reversible airway inflammation with bronchospasm. Mild persistent: symptoms >2x/week (not daily), nocturnal awakening 3-4x/month.
symptoms: Wheeze, breathlessness, chest tightness, cough — often nocturnal or exercise-induced.
first_steps: Low-dose ICS daily + SABA as needed (or SABA + ICS in single inhaler 'reliever'). Avoid known triggers.
red_flags: Severe exacerbation: silent chest, accessory muscle use, SpO2 <92%, peak flow <50% → ER.

name: Upper respiratory tract infection
summary: Viral infection of nose, throat, sinuses. Self-limiting in 7-10 days. >200 viruses, mostly rhinoviruses.
symptoms: Sore throat, runny nose, nasal congestion, mild fever, cough, malaise.
first_steps: Supportive: hydration, rest, acetaminophen/NSAIDs, saline nasal spray. Antibiotics not indicated.
red_flags: Fever >39°C >3 days, severe ear pain, chest pain, dyspnea, stiff neck → re-evaluate (bacterial, pneumonia, meningitis).

name: Acute pharyngitis (strep)
summary: Bacterial throat infection by Group A Streptococcus. Centor criteria: fever, tonsillar exudate, anterior cervical lymphadenopathy, absence of cough.
symptoms: Severe sore throat, fever, painful swallowing, tonsillar exudate, tender nodes.
first_steps: Rapid antigen test or culture; if positive → 10 days oral penicillin V or amoxicillin.
red_flags: Drooling, stridor, voice change → epiglottitis or peritonsillar abscess, ER.

name: Lower back pain (mechanical)
summary: Most common cause of chronic pain. 80% of adults experience it. Usually self-limited (4-6 weeks). 'Mechanical' = related to posture/load, not radicular.
symptoms: Aching pain in lumbar region worsening with movement, often after lifting.
first_steps: Stay active, NSAIDs short course, physical therapy. Imaging only if red flags.
red_flags: Bowel/bladder dysfunction, saddle anesthesia (cauda equina), severe trauma, fever + IV drug use → urgent imaging.

name: Osteoarthritis (knee)
summary: Wear-related cartilage degeneration. Primary OA: idiopathic, age >50, BMI >25.
symptoms: Joint pain worse with use, stiffness <30 min in morning, crepitus, reduced range of motion.
first_steps: Weight loss, quadriceps strengthening, NSAIDs prn, intra-articular steroid for flares. Refer for arthroplasty if conservative measures fail.
red_flags: Acute hot swollen joint with fever → septic arthritis, urgent aspiration.

name: Generalized anxiety disorder
summary: Excessive worry on most days for ≥6 months affecting function. Lifetime prevalence ~5-10%.
symptoms: Restlessness, fatigue, poor concentration, irritability, muscle tension, sleep disturbance.
first_steps: CBT first-line. SSRI (escitalopram, sertraline) or SNRI (venlafaxine). Limit benzodiazepines short-term only.
red_flags: Suicidal ideation, severe functional impairment → urgent psychiatric referral.

name: Major depressive disorder
summary: Persistent sadness or anhedonia + ≥4 other symptoms for ≥2 weeks. PHQ-9 ≥10 indicates moderate.
symptoms: Low mood, anhedonia, sleep/appetite change, fatigue, guilt, poor concentration, suicidal thoughts.
first_steps: CBT or interpersonal therapy. SSRI (sertraline, escitalopram) first-line. Reassess in 4-6 weeks.
red_flags: Active suicidal plan/intent, psychosis, severe self-neglect → emergency psychiatric eval.

name: Iron-deficiency anemia
summary: Most common anemia globally. Microcytic, hypochromic. Confirmed by low ferritin (<30 ng/mL).
symptoms: Fatigue, pallor, dyspnea on exertion, pica, restless legs, koilonychia.
first_steps: Oral iron 65 mg elemental daily for 3 months, take with vitamin C. Investigate cause: GI bleed, menorrhagia, malabsorption.
red_flags: Hb <7 g/dL with symptoms → transfusion. Unexplained iron-deficiency in adult >50 → colonoscopy.

name: Vitamin D deficiency
summary: Serum 25(OH)D <20 ng/mL. Risk: limited sun exposure, dark skin, obesity, malabsorption.
symptoms: Often asymptomatic. May cause bone pain, muscle weakness, fatigue.
first_steps: 50,000 IU vitamin D2 weekly × 8 weeks, then 1000-2000 IU daily maintenance. Recheck at 3 months.
red_flags: Hypocalcemic seizures or tetany → urgent IV calcium + calcitriol.

name: Allergic rhinitis
summary: IgE-mediated nasal inflammation in response to allergens (pollen, dust mites, pet dander).
symptoms: Sneezing, clear rhinorrhea, nasal congestion, itchy eyes, postnasal drip.
first_steps: Avoid trigger. Intranasal steroid (fluticasone, mometasone) is most effective. Add second-gen antihistamine (loratadine, cetirizine).
red_flags: Unilateral symptoms, bloody discharge, severe facial pain → ENT referral.

name: Atopic dermatitis (eczema)
summary: Chronic relapsing pruritic dermatitis with atopic predisposition. Onset usually <5 years old.
symptoms: Dry, itchy, erythematous patches. Children: face/extensors. Adults: flexures.
first_steps: Daily emollients, low/medium-potency topical steroid for flares (1-2 weeks), avoid known triggers.
red_flags: Widespread weeping lesions (eczema herpeticum) → urgent dermatology / ID.

name: Contact dermatitis (allergic)
summary: Type IV hypersensitivity to contact allergen (nickel, fragrance, poison ivy). Patch testing is diagnostic.
symptoms: Erythematous, vesicular, intensely pruritic eruption confined to area of contact.
first_steps: Identify and avoid allergen. Topical steroid 2-3 weeks. Severe → short oral prednisone taper.
red_flags: Widespread or facial involvement, secondary infection.

name: Stable angina
summary: Chest discomfort due to coronary insufficiency, predictable with exertion, relieved by rest or nitroglycerin within 5 min.
symptoms: Substernal pressure radiating to jaw/left arm, dyspnea, diaphoresis.
first_steps: ASA + statin. Beta-blocker first-line antianginal. Sublingual nitroglycerin for episodes. Stress test or angiography.
red_flags: Pain >20 min, at rest, or new onset → ACS, ER.

name: Heart failure (preserved EF)
summary: Symptoms of CHF with LVEF ≥50% and evidence of diastolic dysfunction. Common in elderly with HTN, AF, obesity.
symptoms: Dyspnea on exertion, orthopnea, fatigue, ankle edema.
first_steps: Diuretic for volume overload, SGLT2i (empagliflozin) reduces HF hospitalizations. Aggressive HTN control.
red_flags: Acute decompensation: pulmonary edema → ER.

name: Atrial fibrillation
summary: Most common sustained arrhythmia. Risk: HTN, age, valvular disease, alcohol, hyperthyroidism. Major stroke risk.
symptoms: Palpitations, fatigue, dyspnea — or asymptomatic.
first_steps: Rate vs rhythm control. Stroke prevention: CHA2DS2-VASc ≥2 → DOAC. ECG, TSH, echo workup.
red_flags: Hypotension, chest pain, syncope, new heart failure → urgent rhythm control.

name: Pneumonia (community-acquired)
summary: Lung parenchymal infection in non-hospitalized patient. Most common: S. pneumoniae, M. pneumoniae, viruses.
symptoms: Productive cough, fever, dyspnea, pleuritic chest pain, crackles on exam.
first_steps: Outpatient if CURB-65 ≤1: amoxicillin or doxycycline 5-7 days. Inpatient if hypoxia, sepsis, comorbidity.
red_flags: RR >30, SpO2 <92%, BP <90/60, altered mental status → admit.

name: Acute bronchitis
summary: Self-limiting tracheobronchial inflammation, usually viral. Cough lasts 1-3 weeks.
symptoms: Productive cough, chest discomfort, mild fever, no consolidation on exam.
first_steps: Supportive only — NO antibiotics for uncomplicated viral bronchitis. Reassess if symptoms persist >3 weeks.
red_flags: Dyspnea, hemoptysis, fever >38.5°C with rigors → CXR to rule out pneumonia.

name: Urinary tract infection (uncomplicated)
summary: Bladder infection in healthy non-pregnant women, no urologic anomaly. E. coli most common.
symptoms: Dysuria, urgency, frequency, suprapubic pain. No fever or flank pain.
first_steps: Nitrofurantoin 100 mg BID × 5 days, or fosfomycin single dose. Hydration.
red_flags: Fever, flank pain, vomiting → pyelonephritis, may need IV antibiotics.

name: Hypothyroidism
summary: Underactive thyroid. Most common cause: Hashimoto's autoimmune thyroiditis. Diagnosed by elevated TSH.
symptoms: Fatigue, cold intolerance, constipation, weight gain, dry skin, hair loss, bradycardia.
first_steps: Levothyroxine titrated to TSH 1-2.5 mIU/L. Recheck TSH in 6-8 weeks.
red_flags: Myxedema coma: hypothermia, altered mental status → ICU.

name: Pediatric otitis media
summary: Middle-ear infection in children, usually following URI. Peak age 6-24 months.
symptoms: Ear pain, irritability, fever, decreased hearing, ear pulling in toddlers, possible otorrhea.
first_steps: Watchful waiting 48-72 h if mild and >2 yr. Amoxicillin 80-90 mg/kg/day if severe, bilateral, or <2 yr.
red_flags: Mastoiditis (post-auricular tenderness/erythema), facial palsy → ENT.
